feat: add external newsletter broadcast API

Endpoints for newsletter-pc to send broadcasts via PainClinics.com:
- POST /api/broadcast (create + send/schedule)
- POST /api/broadcast/test (test send to single address)
- GET /api/broadcast/:id (status + analytics)
- DELETE /api/broadcast/:id (cancel queued)
- GET /api/broadcasts (list history)
- GET /api/lists (subscriber lists + counts)

Auth via X-API-Key header, subscriber lists map to contacts tags,
Mailgun delivery with CAN-SPAM unsubscribe footer injection.

feat: busca de imagens e vídeos 100% em português/Brasil

Estratégia de busca redesenhada:
- Claude gera termos em PORTUGUÊS BRASILEIRO (nome popular na academia)
- Google Images com gl=br&hl=pt-BR (localização Brasil)
- Sem restrição CC (elimina sites especializados brasileiros)
- 3 passes: 1) sites BR especializados 2) web BR ampla 3) termo alternativo
- Sites priorizados: musculacao.net, hipertrofia.org, treinoeperformance.com.br,
  dicasdemusculacao.net, gironoticias.com.br, vigorefit.com.br, etc.
- YouTube com regionCode=BR + relevanceLanguage=pt (português brasileiro)
- Badge mostra domínio real do site (🇧🇷 musculacao.net, hipertrofia.org...)
- Fallback sem regionCode se poucos vídeos BR encontrados

commit.message:
Add Changes dashboard: income percentile change arrows

New explore/changes.html shows how homebuyer income percentile ranks
shifted between any two user-selected years. Key features:

- Arrow visualization: rotation (12→3→6 o'clock) shows direction of
  change; green=rising, dark gray=flat, red=falling; saturation scales
  with magnitude. All arrows are drawn on an HTML5 Canvas overlay so
  the map remains a neutral gray choropleth.
- Dual year slider: separate "From" and "To" range inputs let users
  choose any start/end year pair (2007–2024).
- Percentile computation: each geography's median homebuyer income is
  ranked against the national tract distribution for that year using a
  pre-computed CDF (data/income_cdf.json, 110 KB for all 18 years).
- Works at state, county, and census tract zoom levels.
- Hover tooltip shows start/end percentile + income + delta arrow.
- Search, shareable URL hash, and zoom controls match other dashboards.

Supporting files:
- data/income_cdf.json: 1001-quantile income breakpoints per year,
  computed from all U.S. tract data files.
- explore/national.html, income.html: added "By Change" toggle tab.
- explore/index.html: Change Over Time card now links to changes.html.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add scheduled tasks: cron scheduler, CRUD API, and GUI page (#169)

## Summary

- Implements gui/DESIGN.md item 9: scheduled tasks with cron-based
session launching
- Backend: `scheduled_tasks` DB table, asyncio `Scheduler` (30s
interval, croniter), full CRUD router (8 endpoints), shared
`launch_session_subprocess()` function
- Frontend: ScheduledTasks page with table/status badges/actions,
CreateScheduleDialog with cron presets and human-readable descriptions
(cronstrue), query/mutation hooks
- Adds `croniter` (Python) and `cronstrue` (npm) dependencies

## Test plan

- [x] All 125 existing tests pass
- [x] Frontend builds cleanly (tsc + vite)
- [ ] Verify "Schedules" appears in sidebar navigation
- [ ] Create a schedule with cron preset, confirm human-readable
description shows
- [ ] Toggle enable/disable, verify next_run_at updates
- [ ] Wait for schedule to fire, confirm session appears in project
- [ ] Check schedule history shows spawned sessions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve typing accuracy: weighted signals, fairness fixes, stress arrows

- Drop calibration phase: all 25 questions are now LLM-generated adaptive
- Remove behavioral signals from engine, prompts, handler, and frontend
- Add weighted multi-type signals: each option signals 3-5 types with weights
- Add per-type signal coverage tracking to detect neglected types
- Router enforcement: force broad cuts for neglected types after Q8
- Pair question cap (max 3) with inconsistency detection
- Broad cut weight cap to prevent early snowball
- Extend floor protection through Q15
- Add stress arrows and core avoidance to probe guide for all 9 types
- Clean up dashboard: remove behavioral signals and contradictions
- Add simulation script for testing with simulated 6w5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add inline entry price editing to RR details page

- New PATCH /api/rr-watchlist/{uuid}/entry-prices endpoint accepts
  put_option_quote, call_option_quote (and short_call_option_quote for
  Collar) and persists them to the database
- Net entry_price is recalculated: call_quote * call_qty - put_quote * put_qty
  (Collar also subtracts short_call_quote * short_call_qty)
- RR details page gains "Edit Prices" button on the Option Legs card;
  clicking it turns entry price cells into number inputs
- On Save: PATCH API is called, then all dependent values update client-side
  without a page reload: per-leg Change %, Net Entry Price, and the summary
  Change $ / Change % row
- Cancel restores original values; Collar short call leg also editable
- 7 new unit tests covering service and API endpoint; all 379 tests pass

Test Metrics:
- New tests: 7, Updated: 0, Removed: 0
- Ran: 379 tests in ~141s

conversions, payouts, and ERC-1155 transfers.

fix: isolate integration tests from global config file (#727)

Thread a `config_file` parameter through `ClaudeWebUI.__init__` so the
4 config/skills endpoints and `initialize()` use it when set. Both
integration test fixtures now pass an isolated `tmp_path/config.json`,
preventing test runs from reading or mutating `~/.config/cc_webui/config.json`.

Fixes #724

commit.message:
Allow tapping mana sources during may ability prompts

Send manaCost string with MayAbilityMessage so the frontend can
re-evaluate canPay when the mana pool changes. Players can now tap
lands and mana sources while a may ability prompt is showing (e.g.
Origin Spellbomb's "Pay {W} to draw a card?"). The Yes button
dynamically enables once sufficient mana is floating, matching how
X value choice already allows mana tapping.

Frontend parses the mana cost against the current pool on each game
state update, supporting colored, generic, and X costs.

commit.message:
fix: use stdin piping + join output for claude --print in auto-bugfix.ps1

Root cause analysis:
- Positional args and splatting both fail: PowerShell mangles multi-line
  strings when passing as native command arguments
- Stdin piping WORKS: claude receives the full prompt via pipe
- JSON extraction failed because PowerShell captures command output as
  an array of lines, not a single string

Fixes:
1. All 5 claude calls use pipe syntax: $prompt | claude --print ...
2. Scan output joined into single string: (...) -join "`n"
3. JSON regex changed to greedy match: \[\s*\{[\s\S]*\}\s*\]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-09-S56: i_*-failure analysis — extra_kills=1 when rank(i_*)=0 at n=8

KEY FINDINGS:
- HYP-398 CONFIRMED at n=8 for beta_3=1 (30/30 extra_kills=0, rank_i*=1)
- HYP-398 FAILS when rank(i_*)=0 at n=8: extra_kills=1 (3/3 failures)
- b4(T)=0 for ALL 500 b3=1 tournaments at n=7 AND n=8 (seesaw holds for most)
- The cancellation mechanism (old+new face cancellation) breaks at n=8 in rare cases
- This confirms: HYP-398 is equivalent to i_*-injectivity via LES

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: server-side Google OAuth initiation to fix state_mismatch

Cross-origin fetch (vercel.app → railway.app) causes state_mismatch
because the OAuth state cookie set in the cross-origin response is
not properly sent back when Google redirects to the API callback.

Solution: add GET /auth/google on the API that:
1. Makes an internal POST to Better Auth sign-in/social
2. Forwards the Set-Cookie (OAuth state) in a same-site response
3. Redirects the browser to Google

Since the browser navigates directly to railway.app (not via fetch
from vercel.app), the state cookie is set in a same-site context
and is correctly included when Google redirects to /api/auth/callback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Security hardening, bug fixes, and production readiness audit

- Security: HMAC-SHA256 webhook verification for SlickPay with timing-safe comparison
- Security: Timing-safe HMAC comparison for Chargily webhooks (crypto.timingSafeEqual)
- Security: Hashed refresh tokens (SHA-256) — raw tokens never stored in DB
- Security: JWT secrets required in production (requiredInProd pattern)
- Security: Password complexity validation (uppercase, lowercase, digit, special char)
- Security: CSV injection prevention in admin export (prefix formula chars)
- Security: Algerian phone number validation regex

- Auth: Added forgot-password and reset-password endpoints with secure token flow
- Auth: Email normalization (toLowerCase + trim) in all auth schemas
- Auth: Anti-email-enumeration on forgot-password endpoint

- Orders: Fixed race conditions — stock decrement and coupon usage inside Prisma transaction
- Orders: Collision-resistant order numbers (crypto.randomBytes instead of Math.random)
- Cart: Fixed cumulative stock check (existing + new quantity vs available)

- Payments: Fixed SlickPay webhook key mismatch (SLICKPAY → SLICKPAY_EDAHABIA)
- Payments: Fixed manual payment method check (CCP_BARIDIMOB enum match)
- Payments: Added ownership verification on payment status endpoint
- Shipping: Added ownership verification on tracking endpoint

- Middleware: Lazy-init rate limiter (Redis backend with in-memory fallback)
- Middleware: Import-based Prisma error detection (instanceof, not string matching)
- Middleware: Added ZodError and MulterError handling in error handler

- Server: Graceful shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT) with Redis quit + Prisma disconnect
- Server: unhandledRejection and uncaughtException handlers
- Server: Redis connect at startup (non-fatal)

- App: Trust proxy, compression, multi-origin CORS, raw body for webhooks
- Prisma: Added indexes on Order.paymentStatus, Order.couponId, OrderItem.productId
- Prisma: Named CartItem unique constraint

- Docker: Added HEALTHCHECK to Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml
- Docker: Redis maxmemory policy (allkeys-lru, 128mb)
- Docker: Proper entrypoint with exec for signal forwarding
- CI/CD: Added pnpm cache, TypeScript check, JWT env vars, strict lint
- Config: Complete .env.example with all new variables documented
- Docs: Added API.md, DEPLOYMENT.md, PAYMENT_INTEGRATION.md, SHIPPING_INTEGRATION.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix resilience graph amplitude + center prose alignment

Redesign SVG wave with full amplitude cycles (proper ups and downs)
trending diagonally upward — like a macroeconomics growth chart.
Remove all 'broke' labels. Center prose-column for alignment
with infographic elements.

Replace the TODO(M4a) placeholder in concludeByID with type-specific
conclude logic: triggers M4a RunAnalysis (all types), captures M4b
GetPolicySnapshot (bandits), flags surrogate projections, and records
analysis_type per experiment type in the audit trail.

All external calls are best-effort with 5s timeout — conclude completes
even if M4a/M4b are unavailable. Nil clients gracefully degrade to
"skipped_no_client" in audit details.

* feat(m5): PLAYBACK_QOE type validation — require QoE guardrail metrics

Add missing PLAYBACK_QOE validation per design doc §9.3:

Creation: require at least one guardrail config (PLAYBACK_QOE needs
QoE guardrail metrics by definition).

STARTING: validate that at least one metric in the experiment's metric
set (primary, secondary, or guardrail) has is_qoe_metric = true in
metric_definitions. Rolls back to DRAFT on failure.

Adds MetricStore.AnyQoeMetric() for efficient batch QoE check.

4 integration tests: creation without guardrails, start without QoE
metric, valid start, and conclude type details.

Add inline URL mode for small files

commit.message:
Add interactive installer script

Automates full setup on Ubuntu 22.04/24.04:
- Installs system packages (Python 3, MariaDB, Caddy)
- Prompts for domain, admin credentials, DB credentials, and
  optional TV1 / NVD API keys
- Creates the MariaDB database and user
- Clones the repo (or uses existing files), creates venv,
  installs all Python deps
- Generates SECRET_KEY and FERNET_KEY and writes .env (mode 600)
- Adds the Caddy reverse-proxy block and reloads Caddy
- Installs and starts the systemd user service
- Seeds the NVD API key into the DB if provided

commit.message:
Layer 0: Gerbil-compatible reader for Chez Scheme

Implements the Jerboa reader adapted from gherkin's reader with key
changes per the gerbil-like.md design:
- [...] expands directly to (list ...) instead of (@list ...)
- {...} expands to (~ obj 'method args...) instead of (@method ...)
- keyword: syntax using #:name tagged symbols
- Full support: strings, characters, numbers, heredocs, comments,
  vectors, bytevectors, boxes, source locations, pipe symbols,
  syntax quote/quasiquote

65 tests passing.

commit.message:
Fix system fields (display_precision) leaking into view mode

section-view.php had an inline field filter that only checked for tabs,
button_groups, and a regex of known system suffixes — but missed
_display_precision and _vis0 patterns. Replaced with a single call to
SectionRegistry::is_system_field() which is the authoritative system
field detector and covers all SKIP_TYPES, SKIP_KEY_PATTERNS, and
SKIP_NAMES in one place.

commit.message:
Add hamlib and HERMES shared memory radio control to mercury (#29)

* Add hamlib and HERMES shared memory radio control to mercury

Integrate direct PTT keying into mercury via hamlib or HERMES SHM,
eliminating the need for mercury-connector for radio control.

New CLI options:
  -R [model]   HAMLIB radio model (numeric ID)
  -A [address] HAMLIB device file or ip:port
  -S           Use HERMES shared memory interface
  -K           List HAMLIB supported radio models

Default (no flags) is unchanged: PTT state and TCP notifications only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bundle hamlib 4.6.5 Windows x64 DLL and headers for cross-compilation

Adds radio_io/hamlib-w64/ with pre-built DLL, import library and headers
from https://github.com/Hamlib/Hamlib/releases/tag/4.6.5 so that
Windows cross-compilation (make windows) works without a system hamlib
install. Linux builds continue to use the system-installed libhamlib.

Contents:
  bin/   - libhamlib-4.dll + runtime deps (libusb, libgcc, libwinpthread)
  lib/   - libhamlib.dll.a (MinGW import library)
  include/hamlib/ - API headers
  COPYING.LIB.txt - LGPL license

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add windows-zip target to bundle mercury.exe with hamlib DLLs

  make windows-zip

Builds the Windows cross-compiled binary and packages it into
mercury-w64-<git-hash>.zip with all required DLLs (libhamlib-4,
libusb-1.0, libgcc_s_seh-1, libwinpthread-1).

* Add libhamlib-dev to debian build dependencies

Disable the Linux-only HERMES shared-memory radio path on Windows builds and adjust the Windows HAMLIB linker flags to use the bundled import library correctly.

* Backport ffaudio fixes and bump version

Sync the vendored ffaudio copy with the latest upstream fixes for AAudio and PCM conversion/gain handling, and update Mercury's version to 1.9.3.

Add league pagination with Load More button

Support large leagues (50+ members) by paginating standings. New
members are appended to the table and their transfer ratings are
loaded automatically.
